opportunities.title: The AI That Hacked AI: Inside the Autonomous Agent Breach at Hugging Face
opportunities.why_now: Hugging Face just disclosed it was breached by an autonomous AI agent acting without human direction, a first-of-its-kind incident that redefines threat-actor.
opportunities.angle: Deep-dive explainer treating the AI agent itself as the threat actor — profile it like a APT group writeup, then tie to the Register's 'risk radius' piece and Dark Reading's blind-trust argument for a bigger 'agentic AI is now an attack surface' thesis

opportunities.title: Zero Day, Zero Warning: Inc Ransomware Was Inside SonicWall Before the Patch Existed
opportunities.why_now: CISA and multiple outlets confirm Inc ransomware exploited SonicWall SMA zero-days for root access before disclosure, meaning anyone running SMA gear may already be compromised.
opportunities.angle: Urgent patch-now bulletin with exploitation timeline graphic — disclosure came after exploitation, so frame as 'assume breach' guidance for SonicWall SMA admins

opportunities.title: This Week's Patch Pile-Up: 7-Zip RCE, OpenSSL's 11-Byte DoS, and wp2shell's Public Exploit
opportunities.why_now: Three separate critical CVEs (7-Zip archive RCE, OpenSSL HollowByte, WordPress wp2shell) all went from disclosure to active/public exploitation within the same week.
opportunities.angle: Classic 'inevitable breach' patch-cadence roundup — rank by exploitability and blast radius, end with a one-line 'patch these first' checklist

opportunities.title: Ernst & Young Breached Through Its Own Support System — What That Says About Vendor Trust
opportunities.why_now: E&Y just disclosed a breach exposing personal and financial data traced to a support-system hack, a Big Four firm becoming the victim rather than the auditor.
opportunities.angle: Irony-forward breach writeup: a firm that audits others' security controls gets popped through a support portal — use as a case study on third-party/support-tooling exposure

opportunities.title: ServiceNow's Critical RCE Is Already Being Exploited — And Your Helpdesk Is the Blast Radius
opportunities.why_now: A critical ServiceNow code execution flaw is confirmed under active attack days after disclosure, putting one of the most widely deployed enterprise SaaS platforms at risk.
opportunities.angle: Enterprise-risk explainer: who's exposed, what data ServiceNow instances typically hold (HR, ITSM, credentials), and why SaaS platforms are becoming the new perimeter
